mapping.a_element: phases of the coupled electromagnetic field modes in the laser cavity
mapping.b_element: modes of the classical defocusing-NLS wave field

mapping.a_element: pumping rate as the control parameter driving the transition
mapping.b_element: energy density as the control parameter driving the transition

mapping.a_element: ferromagnetic / mode-locked phase (onset of coherent pulse emission)
mapping.b_element: condensate (onset of a large-scale coherent structure)

brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is mean-field spin-glass statistical mechanics of a quenched-disordered Hamiltonian, whose defining outputs are paramagnetic/ferromagnetic/spin-glass phases and an exponentially large number of frozen configurations (a replica/complexity structure); Paper B's machinery is wave-turbulence kinetic theory with a UV cutoff applied to a clean (disorder-free) defocusing NLS, yielding a Bose-Einstein-like, subcritical, dimension-dependent condensation. Both apply equilibrium statistical mechanics to nonlinearly-coupled optical modes and find a coherence phase transition, but that overlap is generic: A's central disorder/spin-glass/complexity apparatus has no counterpart in B, and B's wave-turbulence condensation apparatus has no counterpart in A, so the governing methods differ.
